Patch Set 1:

Frank, junits failed.   I think though this work is better linked to JIRA POLICY-52 rather than creating POLICY-69.   What do you think, Pam? As a note, POLICY-69 has typos in title ("added junit testing jave files").   Please change, pending to hear from Pam, I think you should be using existing POLICY-52 though.
Regardless, need to make an amend on this commit to fix the tests.

Patch Set 1:

Yes POLICY-52 is meant for this work.

Patch Set 1:

Error seems related to an h2 database lock timeout condition.   There has some outage on LF servers, so wondering that's real root cause, better scenario to deal with that if it turned out to be a timing issue.    We'll see.  Kicking the verify job again, now that there is reports that the power outage issues has been mitigated (not totally).

Patch Set 1:

Frank, it seems that your new tests have uncovered a bug, perhaps due to timing conditions, that we were not hitting before.   Note that other in progress changes, specifically, Lasksman changes, another in progress code inspection, builds ECOMP-PAP-REST OK against the same base (it fails in some other issue that you will eventually hit).   See below progress passing PAP-REST module in https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/5757/1:
